entries.title: Coruscant New Republic Capital but Scarred

content.fact: Once liberated, Coruscant becomes the New Republic capital but remains politically scarred by Imperial rule.
content.injection: Use Coruscant as a bureaucratic and symbolic center with lingering Imperial infrastructure and intrigue. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Coruscant Liberated.
Respect story-position bounds from Coruscant Liberated to Coruscant Liberated.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Yavin IV Praxeum Is Sacred and Dangerous

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Yavin IV becomes Luke’s Jedi training site while carrying ancient dark-side danger from Exar Kun.
content.injection: During the Jedi Academy founding era, make Yavin IV both hopeful and hazardous: Luke is rebuilding Jedi training there, but ancient temple danger and Exar Kun-linked shadow can pressure scenes.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Wayland / Mount Tantiss Is Hidden Imperial Legacy

content.fact: Wayland hides Mount Tantiss, cloning resources, and Imperial secrets that shape the Thrawn campaign.
content.injection: Before discovery, keep Wayland/Tantiss as secret; after discovery, use it as a dangerous Imperial legacy site.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Byss Belongs to Dark Empire Context

content.fact: Byss is a hidden dark-side Imperial throne world in Dark Empire, not common New Republic geography.
content.injection: When active in Dark Empire crisis, only surface Byss as active lore in Dark Empire or later contexts, not routine post-Endor scenes. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Empire crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Empire Crisis Begins to Palpatine Clone Threat Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Dathomir Is a Force Clan World

content.fact: Dathomir contains clan-based Force traditions, rancors, and local power structures distinct from galactic politics.
content.injection: Use Dathomir as a dangerous local culture with its own rules rather than a generic wilderness planet. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han and Leia Married.
Respect story-position bounds from Han and Leia Married to Han and Leia Married.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Hapes Court Applies Marriage and Alliance Pressure

content.fact: Hapes operates through court power, isolation, wealth, and dynastic politics.
content.injection: In Hapan context, diplomacy often carries personal and marriage politics with strategic consequences. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han and Leia Married.

entries.title: Mon Calamari as Dark Empire Target

content.fact: Mon Calamari becomes a symbolic and strategic target during World Devastator attacks.
content.injection: During Dark Empire, attacks on Mon Calamari should read as terror against a major Rebel/New Republic-aligned world.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: World Devastators Attack.

context.label: Mid New Republic crises
context.phase: Mid New Republic to Hand of Thrawn Era

content.fact: Corellia has strong local identity and later crisis potential that can conflict with central New Republic authority.
content.injection: In Corellian contexts, emphasize local pride, autonomy, and political friction with Coruscant. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mid New Republic crises.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
